paper_title: Geochemical Analysis of Core Samples from Langgu Sag

methods.description: Process for collecting, cleaning, and preparing core samples for geochemical analysis to minimize diagenesis impact.

steps.description: Fifty core samples of the Es were collected from exploration wells in the Langgu sag.
steps.searchable_summary: collect core samples from exploration wells

steps.description: The pure dark gray mudstone samples without veins were carefully sampled.
steps.searchable_summary: select pure dark gray mudstone samples

steps.description: All the rock samples are cleaned and crushed into powder prior to the experiments.
steps.searchable_summary: clean and crush rock samples into powder

methods.description: Procedure to determine the total organic carbon content in rock samples using a LECO CS230 carbon sulfur analyzer.

steps.description: About 100 mg of rock powder is treated with hydrochloric acid to remove inorganic carbon in carbonates.
steps.searchable_summary: treat rock powder with HCl to remove inorganic carbon

steps.description: The acid treated sample is saturated with distilled water to a neutral state and dried for 24 h.
TOC analysis is completed under the LECO CS230 carbon sulfur analyzer.

methods.description: Procedure to perform Rock-Eval pyrolysis on rock samples using the Rock-Eval OGE-II instrument.

steps.description: Rock-Eval pyrolysis is carried out using the Rock-Eval OGE-II instrument.

methods.description: Procedure to analyze stable isotopes in rock samples to determine carbon and oxygen isotopic compositions.

steps.description: A split of each source rock sample is decalcified by HCl and combusted at 875°C to generate CO2.
steps.searchable_summary: decalcify and combust rock samples to generate CO2

steps.description: Determine the carbon isotopic compositions of the organic fraction (δ13C OM) on a Finnigan MAT 253 mass spectrometer.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze δ13C OM using Finnigan MAT 253

steps.description: Splits of these rock samples are pretreated with H2O2 to eliminate the influence of organics.
Pretreated samples are treated with phosphoric acid at 70°C for at least 3 h to release CO2.
steps.searchable_summary: treat pretreated samples with phosphoric acid to release CO2

steps.description: The analysis of collected CO2 is performed on a Finnigan MAT 253 mass spectrometer.

methods.description: Procedure to analyze trace elements in mudstone rock samples using ICP-MS.

steps.description: Mudstone rock samples are reacted with HNO3/HF (1:2) and heated at a temperature of 185°C for 24 h.
steps.searchable_summary: react mudstone samples with HNO3/HF and heat

steps.description: The measurement of trace elements is completed with an ICP-MS.

methods.description: Procedure to determine major element contents in samples using a wavelength dispersive X-ray fluorescence spectrometer.

steps.description: Samples are dissolved with Li2B4O7, NH4NO3, and LiF.
steps.searchable_summary: dissolve samples with Li2B4O7, NH4NO3, and LiF

steps.description: Measured by a wavelength dispersive X-ray fluorescence spectrometer.
steps.searchable_summary: measure major elements using X-ray fluorescence spectrometer

methods.description: Procedure to analyze bulk mineral compositions of rock samples using XRD.

steps.description: The remaining powder samples are selected for XRD analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: select powder samples for XRD analysis

steps.description: This experiment is carried out on a Bruker D2 PHASER diffractometer system.
steps.searchable_summary: perform XRD analysis using Bruker D2 PHASER
